content.full_text:
L.G. Statham

March 25, 1990

FPPC
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Margaret Ellison, Legal Counsel

Dear Ms. Elison

I wish a legal opinion on the following facts:

A non-profit corporation is to be formed. It will be a 501(3c for educational purposes. It will not be controlled by a candidate or office holder. It will not be run on a day to day basis by a candidate or office holder. It's name will not incorporate the name of a candidate or office holder. It will not lobby. It will not make contributions to candidates or committees for initiative purposes. Their will be 3 directors; two public members and the sponse of a candidate/office holder who will also be an employee of the corporation.

The questions are this?

1.     Can an office holder solicit money on behalf of the corporation?

2.     Can an office holder donate his services as an announcer on behalf of the corporation?

I would appreciate a reply within 10 days if you possibly could.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Sincerely,

L.G. Statham
1008 10th St. #514
Sacramento, CA 95814

sections.question: A non-profit corporation is to be formed. It will be a 501(c)(3) for educational purposes. It will not be controlled by a candidate or office holder. It will not be run on a day to day basis by a candidate or office holder. It's name will not incorporate the name of a candidate or office holder. It will not lobby. It will not make contributions to candidates or committees for initiative purposes. Their will be 3 directors; two public members and the spouse of a candidate/office holder who will also be an employee of the corporation. The questions are this: 1. Can an office holder solicit money on behalf of the corporation? 2. Can an office holder donate his services as an announcer on behalf of the corporation?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an office holder can solicit funds on behalf of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation and whether an office holder can donate personal services as an announcer to such a corporation, given the specified organizational structure and constraints.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section is present. Minor spelling variations noted (Elison/Ellison, L99O/1990, solict/solicit, Their/There). Document contains the question but no FPPC legal opinion.
